rules.standard: Children's safety duties — highly effective age assurance
rules.intent: Where a service can be accessed by children and carries content harmful to them, 'highly effective' age assurance is required — self-declaration alone does not meet the bar.
rules.fix: Integrate a recognised age-assurance method (age estimation, ID/age verification, or equivalent) on the access path.

rules.intent: Users must be able to easily report illegal content and content harmful to children on user-to-user surfaces (chat, livestream, profiles).
rules.fix: Add a report/flag affordance to every user-generated-content surface, wired to a complaints workflow.

rules.intent: Users (especially children) must be able to block and mute other users on interactive surfaces.
rules.fix: Provide block/mute controls on chat, livestream, and profile surfaces.

rules.intent: Proportionate content-moderation measures should be applied to user-generated content (filtering, profanity/abuse detection, or moderation hooks).
rules.fix: Add moderation hooks (profanity/abuse filtering, content classification, or a moderation queue) to UGC surfaces.

rules.intent: Services must carry out and keep up to date an illegal-content risk assessment (and, where in scope, a children's access/risk assessment).
rules.fix: Add an illegal-content (and children's) risk assessment artifact to the repo or link it from governance docs.

rules.intent: There must be a route to detect, remove, and report child sexual abuse material (CSEA), e.g. referral to NCMEC/IWF.
rules.fix: Document and implement a CSAM detection/escalation route (NCMEC/IWF referral or equivalent) for UGC.

rules.intent: Terms of service / community guidelines must clearly prohibit illegal content and set out enforcement.
rules.fix: Add community guidelines / acceptable-use terms that prohibit illegal content and describe enforcement.

assert.find: prohibited content|illegal content|community guidelines|acceptable use|content policy
